title: strychnos nux-vomica
intro: Strychnos nux-vomica, also known as the poison nut tree or strychnine tree, is a highly toxic plant native to India and Southeast Asia. Traditionally, the seeds of this plant have been used in medicine for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, as well as their ability to treat snake bites and other venomous attacks. However, its medicinal properties are often overshadowed by its deadly toxicity, making it a double-edged sword in herbal remedies. In some traditional herbal preparations, the seeds are dried and ground into a powder, which can be used to treat conditions such as rheumatism and fever, but with extreme caution and under the guidance of a medical professional.
